page: OVHcloud b3-8 is a general-purpose virtual server hosted on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC processor running at 2.3 GHz. The instance is configured with 2 dedicated physical cores and 2 vCPUs with no hyperthreading, providing a dedicated allocation of resources. It includes 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core, and comes bundled with 50 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Network connectivity includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks reveal a strong single-core profile but limited multi-core scaling. The server achieves top 25% results in stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Geekbench single-core scores, alongside strong text processing capabilities. However, multi-core benchmarks, such as the Geekbench multi-core score and Passmark CPU score, fall into the bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth is mixed, showing average performance for large uncached blocks but weak performance for small cached blocks.

Due to its dedicated CPU allocation and balanced vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the b3-8 offers predictable single-threaded performance for its hardware class. However, its low core count and weak multi-core scaling make it poorly suited for parallelized tasks like software compilation, ray tracing, or medium-to-large LLM inference. It is best utilized for lightweight general-purpose workloads, single-threaded applications, basic text processing, and small-scale web serving where dedicated CPU resources are required without massive parallel processing needs.
description: The OVHcloud b3-8 is a general-purpose virtual server featuring 2 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs running at 2.3 GHz on an x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 8.0 GB of RAM, 50 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and runs on a KVM hypervisor. Benchmarks indicate strong single-core performance, with stress-ng and Geekbench single-core scores placing in the top 25%. However, multi-core performance and memory bandwidth are weaker, placing in the bottom 25% for tasks like software compilation and database operations. This server is highly cost-efficient for workloads requiring dedicated, predictable single-threaded CPU performance rather than heavy parallel processing. It is best suited for lightweight general-purpose applications, basic text processing, and small-scale web serving.
og_description: OVHcloud b3-8 is a general-purpose KVM virtual server with 2 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and 50 GB NVMe SSD, offering strong single-core performance for lightweight workloads.
meta_description: OVHcloud b3-8 is a general-purpose KVM server with 2 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and 50 GB NVMe SSD, optimized for single-core tasks.
tagline: A dedicated dual-core virtual server offering strong single-threaded performance and local NVMe storage for lightweight general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 2 dedicated AMD EPYC physical cores at 2.3 GHz
Equipped with 8.0 GB RAM and 50 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Delivers strong single-core CPU and text processing performance
Exhibits weaker multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth under heavy parallel loads
Best suited for lightweight general-purpose applications and single-threaded tasks
